claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children that I'm inquiring about. Their names are Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you determine eligibility for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

Let me start by asking: **How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 3 years old, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! So Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2 - all under 16.

Next question: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your living arrangements with the children.

**Do Alex, Blake, and Charlie all live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, all three of them live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, or Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

**Are any of the children currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for extended periods, or are they fostered or adopted?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation, and they're not fostered or adopted. They all just live here with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- All three children are under 16 years old
- They all live with you in the UK
- You are responsible for their care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them
- There are no special circumstances affecting their eligibility

You meet all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. 

**Important note:** Please be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. Even if this applies to you, you can still claim Child Benefit, but you may need to pay back some or all of it through your tax return.

If you'd like to proceed with claiming, you can make a claim for Child Benefit through the official government process. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Alex's care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there are no special circumstances (hospital, care, fostering, etc.) that would affect eligibility. The claimant meets all the standard criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Blake's care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and there are no special circumstances (hospital, care, fostering, etc.) that would affect eligibility. The claimant meets all the standard criteria for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 2 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Charlie's care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, and there are no special circumstances (hospital, care, fostering, etc.) that would affect eligibility. The claimant meets all the standard criteria for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with three children: Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2). All three children are under 16 years old and live with the claimant. The claimant is responsible for their care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for any of the children. There are no special circumstances affecting any of the children. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for all three children.

The claimant meets all the standard criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

The claimant meets all the standard criteria for Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with three children: Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2). All three children are under 16 years old and live with the claimant. The claimant is responsible for their care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for any of the children. There are no special circumstances affecting any of the children. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for all three children.
